jobs.title: Jobs register & change-tracking traceability
jobs.description: Add a SpecPad-owned jobs register (jobs.json) with an open/closed lifecycle, the active-job governance rule, the editor Jobs view and active-job picker, and the skill git plumbing so every spec change traces to a job. Baselined at v1.1.

jobs.title: Onboarding: specpad init
jobs.description: One-command setup that scaffolds docs/specpad/, installs the pre-push hook, wires the SpecPad working-loop directive into the project CLAUDE.md, and generates the launcher — so a new user is fully set up on install with no manual configuration. Idempotent and non-destructive.

jobs.title: Baseline generator (draft a spec from existing code)
jobs.description: An agent procedure that bootstraps a draft v1 SRS/VTP from an existing codebase — surveying modules, public behavior, and existing tests — so SpecPad can be adopted on a project that did not start with it. Output is a draft for human ratification, mapped to existing tests, governance-clean.

jobs.title: Requirement audit (reconcile spec with code)
jobs.description: An agent procedure that reconciles the existing SRS/VTP against the whole codebase, reporting drift both directions — code behavior with no requirement (missing), requirements whose behavior is gone/changed (stale), and requirements/test references with no covering test (coverage). Reports ratifiable proposals; applies nothing destructive. The commit-time audit is its staged-diff form.

jobs.title: Spec reconciliation (apply requirement-audit findings)
jobs.description: Apply the ratified findings from the first requirement audit: add the missing recent-projects requirement (EDS-12) mapped to its existing tests, correct the stale ValidationPanel test reference (TEST-22 -> StatusBar), and close two of the four not_tested coverage gaps (TEST-9 id-immutability, TEST-59 records-only) with real tests.

jobs.title: Jobs view as release notes
jobs.description: Reframe the editor Jobs tab as release notes: group jobs by major version (unversioned under Unreleased), then by type into Features and Bugfixes, with each job's description beneath its title. Clicking a job opens a separate detail view. Adds the job record type and version fields and moves Jobs to the leftmost tab.

jobs.title: Closed-job change cache, owner, derived version, in-progress vs released views
jobs.description: On close, cache a job's before/after spec snapshots (lockfile-class) so the browser editor can show its SRS/VTP changes; add an owner set from git at creation; derive a job's version from the release tag containing its commits; split the Jobs tab into In progress (open) and Released (closed) views.

jobs.title: Source-code traceability (job to commits to code)
jobs.description: Cache each closed job's commit list (commits.json, derived from the Job: trailer) so the editor shows a job's commits alongside its SRS/VTP changes, and document the skill's source-traceability export (per-job commits + code diffs from git) that feeds the eQMS evidence package.

jobs.title: Architecture spec (arc42 + C4) and Architecture view
jobs.description: Add an optional architecture spec — an arc42 software architecture document (markdown) plus a C4 model in Structurizr DSL — tracked under docs/specpad/, job/release coupled (no requirement-level trace matrix). New editor Architecture view renders the arc42 doc and presents the C4 DSL. Dogfooded with SpecPad's own SAD.

jobs.title: Architecture templates (generic + medical), init path quiz, beefed-up generic SAD
jobs.description: Ship two architecture profiles — a beefed-up generic arc42+C4 template (several diagrams, no safety classification) and a medical template (62304/FDA: per-unit classification, segregation, verification) — each with a soft authoring guide. The init quiz lets the user pick generic vs medical and scaffolds the match. Re-dogfood SpecPad's SAD with the richer generic template. Cybersecurity architecture is a noted coming-soon pillar.

jobs.title: Architecture Edit/Display view: CodeMirror editor, markdown rendering, diagram SVG
jobs.description: Give the Architecture tab Edit/Display sub-tabs: a syntax-highlighting CodeMirror editor for the arc42 markdown and C4 source, and a Display that renders the markdown as HTML (react-markdown) with inline diagram SVGs (draw.io export) and the authoring-guide panel. Diagram change tracking stays coarse (file changed: yes/no). draw.io is the pragmatic diagram source; SVG export is rendered client-side.

jobs.title: Diagram-heavy architecture: markdown-placed inline diagrams; Structurizr DSL optional
jobs.description: Let the arc42 markdown define where diagrams go via image references; the Display renders each referenced SVG inline at its place. Ship several diagrams (Context overview near the top, Building Block interfaces, Runtime process, Deployment) and demote the Structurizr/C4 DSL to an optional alternative (loader kept, editor shows it only when present, removed from default templates + SpecPad's dogfood).

jobs.title: Architecture changes in the job detail (coarse file-level + SAD line diff)
jobs.description: Snapshot the architecture files (sad.md, diagrams, guide, optional dsl) into the closed-job cache, and show an Architecture changes section in the job detail: which arch files were added/modified/removed (coarse), plus an added/removed-line diff for the SAD markdown. Completes SAD-4. Diagrams stay coarse (no in-diagram deltas).

jobs.title: Split into core specpad + optional specpad-medical add-on skill
jobs.description: Extract medical/regulatory content into a separate specpad-medical add-on skill (62304/FDA profile, medical template + guide, regulatory governance), keeping core specpad lean and general (generic profile, contract, jobs, working loop). The contract and editor stay unified (medical features light up only when a medical profile is present). Controls bloat: the regulated layer's token cost is paid only when opted in.

jobs.title: Formal Release entity: release-as-jobs view + full-doc release snapshot
jobs.description: Make a release first-class: a release is its set of jobs at a version, with a guaranteed full snapshot of all key docs and a Releases view that reads as release notes (jobs grouped per release). Core feature. Dogfooded by cutting v1.2.

jobs.title: Product requirements (PRD) register + integrations design addendum
jobs.description: Add an optional product-requirements (PRD) register as an SRS-shaped peer document, with upward SRS→PRD traceability (satisfies, by id) and opt-in governance (prd-referential-integrity, prd-coverage) that costs nothing without a PRD register. Capture the integrations design addendum (Jira/Confluence ingest add-on, import vs baseline routing, multi-repo system-level docs, PRD/validation, usability/interface-spec) as forward design. Dogfooded: SpecPad gains its own PRD register and SRS→PRD links.

jobs.title: Auditor view — live design-control evidence (coverage, trace matrix, gaps)
jobs.description: Add an Auditor view (editor tab) that renders a reviewer's-eye picture of the open project: a coverage summary (requirements verified, test results, product-requirement coverage), the PRD→SRS→VTP traceability matrix with per-requirement verification results, and a gap list derived from the shared governance check plus the proposed-PRD roadmap. Load the optional PRD register into the editor so product requirements and upward traces are available. Includes a scope disclaimer (evidence that supports design controls; not a QMS / compliance determination).

jobs.title: Editor visual system: design tokens, themes, Overview landing, tab reorder
jobs.description: Polish the editor into a cohesive, modern product. Build a CSS design-token system and ship three selectable themes (developer tool, editorial, blueprint) with a menubar switcher persisted to localStorage (applied before first paint, no flash). Add an Overview (Home) landing — an engineer's status home showing in-progress jobs, the latest release, and a coverage/health headline, with quick navigation into the authoring views — and make it the default view. Reorder the document tabs to lead with Overview then the authoring views, grouping the oversight views (Auditor, Releases, Jobs) after; this supersedes JV-4's leftmost-Jobs rule (changed under this job, not JOB-6).
